full_text:
Post Xenium t-CycIF protocol

Note:

According to this protocol Xenium slides can be stored up to 2 weeks at 4°C: https://www.protocols.io/view/ffpe-tissue-processing-for-multimodal-imaging-assa-q26g71rwqgw z/v1 . This protocol was made by merging the above protocol for Xenium slides handling with our in house tCycIF protocol.

Materials:

General Reagents and Materials:

10 X PBS (Fisher BioReagents cat: BP399500 ) (PBS)

Glycerol (Molecular Biology Grade, Liquid, ≥99.0% cat:G5516-1L )

Milli-Q water

Antigen Retrieval buffer pH6 (citric acid Monohydrate, Sigma Cat: C1909 1L) Coplin Jars

Coverslip 24 X 60mm #0 (Fisher scientific, cat:BB02400600A033MNZ0)

Bleaching Solution

1X PBS diluted from 10X Fisher BioReagents cat: BP399500

Hydrogen Peroxide 30% (w/v) Extra Pure SLR , Fisher Scientific , cat: H1750/15 Sodium Hydroxide Pellets , Sigma-Aldrich, cat: 31434-1KG-M

Instrumentations:

Rarecyte Cytefinder II , Rarecyte inc

Antigen retrieval chamber, Biocare Medical

tCycIF Protocol after Xenium

1.1At the end of the Xenium run, remove the slide cassette assembly from the Xenium Analyzer. 1.2The slide can be processed immediately or stored at Temperature4 °C for up to 2 weeks.

Slide Storage

2

For slide storage:

2.1 After the Xenium run, remove the liquid covering the slide. Add 1 mL PBS and incubate for 00:05:00 minutes at Room temperature.

2.2 Remove PBS. Add 1 mL storage solution (50% glycerol in PBS) to the slide. Replace the lid to seal the cassette and store for up to 2 weeks at 4 °C

Note: The slides were kept in the fridge when it was not in use .

Eg: Wait time between bleaching and Antibody addition around 3 hours , at which time slides were in fridge.

Tissue processing for Phenocycler-Fusion assay

3 Tissue processing can start directly after Xenium run or from a previously stored slide

3.1 Processing slide directly after Xenium run:

■ After the Xenium run, remove the liquid from the slide and remove slide from the cassette assembly

■ Wash slide in PBS for 00:05:00 mins Room temperature. Repeat for a total of two washes

■ Wash slide in DI or Milli-Q water for 00:02:00 mins Room temperature

■ Proceed to step 4

7m

3.2

Processing slide from storage:

■ Remove storage solution from the tissue and remove slide from the cassette assembly ■ Wash slide with PBS for 00:05:00 mins Room temperature. Repeat for a total of four washes

■ Wash slide in DI or MilliQ water for 00:02:00 mins Room temperature

■ Proceed to step 4

4 Perform antigen retrieval to de-crosslink and reveal epitopes. Antigen retrieval can be performed in citrate buffer (pH 6.0)

● Add 550 ml of MQ water to the chamber. Inside the chamber are 3 rack holders, add about 225 ml Citric acid buffer to the rack holder where you intend to place slides, add MQ water if you don’t intend to place slides.

● Now place the slides in the rack holder containing Citric acid buffer.Tighten the lid firmly then turn the device on and select preset settings from saved protocol of 950C without preheat.Which is the 3rd row in saved protocols.The total time takes around 45 minutes.

● After the protocol is over, open the lid carefully(use heat resistance gloves) and transfer the slides and citric acid buffer to a new plastic rack holder. Take it to the hood to cool it faster. 20 minutes should be enough for the temperature to cool down to 400C.

● Once the slides have cooled down, wash three times with 1x PBS, ~ 3 min each.

Prepare a moist incubation chamber to minimize evaporation: Wet regular tissue paper under the tap water and fold it to fit inside the grey slide box. After cooling and washing from step 1.2., take out the slides one at a time, dry the surroundings of the tissue section using soft fiber-free tissue paper. Without letting the tissue dry, quickly add 200-300 uL Odyssey blocking buffer per section and incubate for 30 minutes at Room Temperature.

5 Bleaching/Fluorophore inactivation

Prepare 50 mL bleaching solution for 8 slides in a falcon tube as follows:

40 ml 1xPBS

7.5ml 30% H2O2

2.5ml 480 mM NaOH

5.1. Pour the bleaching solution into a white slide box lid placed on top of an LED light. 5.2. Using forceps, transfer the slides into a bleaching solution; make sure that the slides are completely immersed in the solution. Place another LED light on top of the slide box (step 3.2). Connect bottom and top LED lights to power.

5.3. Photo bleach the sides for 70 min at RT, during which change the buffer solution after every 20 min. NOTE: Only for cycles 0 The rest of the cycles are only 1x after 25 min.

5.4. Wash four times in PBS, ~ 3 min each.

Staining with Conjugated antibodies :

Stain o/n with antibodies .

Repeat steep 5 for further cycles

Notes for the scanning:

We are unable to capture with our laser wavelengths the xenium markers

Xenium slides are thinner than superfrost. Use N=110, range=140 in your assay for 20x scanning

Full xenium areas take around 15 minutes, 4 channels at 20x

20x unbinned recommended, change binning to bin=1 instead of the usual bin=2

5um thick look bit more crowded than usual 4um cycif slides

Do not attach any stickers to the slides

Be careful with coverslips, they might be more wobbly than usual cycif

The slides are very transparent, in all steps read the label before moving, wiping the slide
